data.headline: BuiltWell CT in Orange, Connecticut
data.subheadline: Headquartered at 206A Boston Post Road in Orange, CT — serving homeowners across Fairfield and New Haven Counties with full-service remodeling.

data.title: About Our Orange, CT Office
data.content:
BuiltWell CT has been headquartered in Orange, Connecticut since the company's founding. Our office at 206A Boston Post Road serves as the base of operations for every project we take on across both Fairfield and New Haven Counties. This central location gives our teams efficient access to job sites throughout southern Connecticut — from Greenwich to Madison and everywhere in between.

Every project is managed by our in-house team, not subcontracted out. We hold a Connecticut Home Improvement Contractor license (#0668405) and carry full liability and workers' compensation insurance. Being based in Orange means we're deeply familiar with the building departments, permit processes, and housing stock across both counties we serve.

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling
items.description: Full kitchen renovations including cabinetry, countertops, tile, electrical, plumbing, and permits.

items.title: Bathroom Remodeling

items.title: Basement Finishing
items.description: Convert unfinished basements into living space with proper waterproofing and egress.

items.title: Flooring
items.description: Hardwood, LVP, tile, and engineered wood with expert subfloor preparation.

items.title: Home Additions
items.description: Room additions, bump-outs, and second-story additions with full structural engineering and permitting.

items.title: Interior Painting
items.description: Professional interior painting with proper prep, premium paints, and clean lines throughout.

data.title: Recent New Haven County Projects
data.subtitle: A sample of recent work completed from our Orange office for homeowners in New Haven County.

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in Milford
items.description:
A Milford homeowner needed to replace dated 1990s oak cabinets and laminate countertops. We removed the peninsula, installed soft-close cabinetry with quartz countertops and subway tile, and extended LVP flooring into the dining room.

"They showed up when they said they would, cleaned up every day, and the kitchen turned out better than I imagined." — Ivana P., Milford

items.title: Whole-Home Remodeling in Hamden
items.description:
A Hamden family purchased a 1960s colonial and needed a complete renovation — kitchen, two bathrooms, new flooring throughout, and a finished basement. We managed the entire project from a single contract with coordinated scheduling across all trades.

"Having one contractor handle everything made all the difference. The house feels completely new." — The Nguyen Family, Hamden

items.title: Bathroom Remodeling in Orange
items.description:
An Orange homeowner wanted to update a dated 1970s bathroom with pink tile and a fiberglass tub surround. We gutted the space, installed a frameless glass walk-in shower with porcelain tile, a floating vanity with quartz top, and new plumbing fixtures throughout.

"We finally have a bathroom that feels modern. The tile work is flawless and the shower is exactly what we wanted." — Mark & Lisa R., Orange

data.title: Why We're Based in Orange, CT
data.content:
Orange sits at the crossroads of Fairfield and New Haven Counties — a practical, well-maintained town that reflects the values we bring to every project.

Orange is a residential community of roughly 14,000 residents along the Boston Post Road corridor between Milford and New Haven. The town's housing stock is predominantly split-level and colonial homes built between the 1950s and 1980s — the same construction types we work in every day across both counties.

Orange homeowners are practical and value-oriented. They invest in their homes thoughtfully, prioritize quality workmanship, and expect clear communication throughout the process. That mindset aligns with how we operate — no shortcuts, no surprises, and honest pricing from the start.

Our location on Boston Post Road gives us direct access to I-95, the Merritt Parkway, and Route 34, making it easy to reach job sites anywhere from Greenwich to Madison within 45 minutes. Being local also means we know the Orange building department, the town's inspection processes, and the specific conditions these homes present.

data.title: Remodeling in Orange, Connecticut
data.content:
We know Orange homes because we've been working in them since day one.

Orange's residential neighborhoods are dominated by split-level and colonial homes built between the 1950s and 1980s. These homes share common characteristics: original electrical panels that may need upgrading for modern loads, plaster walls in older builds, asbestos floor tiles in basements and kitchens, and plumbing that predates current code requirements. We assess all of these conditions during our initial visit and account for them in every proposal.

Homeowners in Orange tend to be practical and value-oriented. They want quality work done right the first time, clear pricing with no hidden costs, and a contractor who shows up when scheduled. That's how we've built our business — and being headquartered here keeps us accountable to the community we see every day.

The Orange building department has straightforward permit processes, and we maintain a strong working relationship with their inspection team. Whether your project requires building, electrical, or plumbing permits — or all three — we handle every application and coordinate every inspection as part of the project scope.

Many Orange homeowners are also updating homes they've lived in for decades. Kitchen and bathroom renovations are the most common starting points, often followed by basement finishing, flooring updates, or whole-home projects that address multiple rooms in a single coordinated effort.

data.title: Our Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every project follows the same five-step process. This structure keeps projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home or connect via Google Meet or Zoom to discuss your goals, assess the space, and answer your questions. No charge. No obligation. We look at the layout, note the existing electrical and plumbing conditions, and get a clear picture of what you want to accomplish.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering exactly what's included, how long it will take, and what it costs. Line items are specific. We break out cabinetry, countertops, tile, electrical, plumbing, flooring, and permits separately so you understand exactly where the budget is going.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through material choices with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so selections are made on schedule before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. You receive daily updates on progress, a clean job site at the end of every workday, and crews who arrive when scheduled. If something unexpected comes up behind a wall, we contact you that day and present your options.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished project together. We check every cabinet, every drawer, every tile, every light fixture. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete. Your written acceptance at the final walkthrough is the last step.

data.title: Our Orange Office
data.description: Our headquarters is open for consultations, material discussions, and project planning.
data.headline: Our Orange Office

data.title: Serving Two Counties from Orange
data.subtitle: From our Orange headquarters, we serve homeowners throughout Fairfield and New Haven Counties with dedicated teams in both regions.

items.question: Where is your office located and can I visit?
items.answer: Our office is located at 206A Boston Post Road in Orange, CT 06477. We're open Monday through Friday from 8am to 5pm and Saturday from 8am to 3pm. You're welcome to stop by during business hours to discuss a project. We recommend calling ahead at (203) 466-9148 so we can make sure someone is available to sit down with you and give you our full attention.

items.question: What areas do you serve from your Orange office?
items.answer: From our Orange headquarters, we serve homeowners throughout both Fairfield County and New Haven County. Our New Haven County service area includes Orange, New Haven, Hamden, Milford, West Haven, Branford, Guilford, Madison, Woodbridge, North Haven, East Haven, Cheshire, Wallingford, and surrounding towns. Our Fairfield County service area covers Greenwich, Stamford, Norwalk, Westport, Fairfield, Darien, New Canaan, Ridgefield, and surrounding communities.

items.question: How do I schedule a consultation?
items.answer: You can schedule a free consultation by calling (203) 466-9148 for New Haven County or (203) 919-9616 for Fairfield County. You can also fill out the contact form on this page or use the scheduling tool to pick a date and time that works for you. We offer both in-person and remote consultations via Google Meet. There is no charge and no obligation.

items.question: Are you licensed and insured?
items.answer: Yes. BuiltWell CT holds Connecticut Home Improvement Contractor license #0668405. We carry full general liability insurance and workers' compensation coverage. We are happy to provide copies of our insurance certificates upon request before any project begins.

items.question: How long does a typical remodeling project take?
items.answer: Timelines vary by project scope. A bathroom remodel typically takes 3 to 6 weeks. Kitchen remodels run 6 to 12 weeks depending on complexity. Basement finishing averages 4 to 8 weeks. We provide a detailed schedule in every written proposal so you know exactly what to expect before work begins.

items.question: Do you handle permits and inspections?
items.answer: Yes. Permit coordination is included in every project scope. We handle all building, electrical, and plumbing permit applications and schedule every required inspection with the Orange building department or whichever municipality your project is in. You never need to visit town hall or coordinate with inspectors yourself.

items.question: What does your proposal include?
items.answer: Every BuiltWell proposal is a detailed, line-item document that breaks out costs for each component of the project: cabinetry, countertops, tile, plumbing, electrical, flooring, permits, and labor. There are no hidden fees. You see exactly where the budget is going before you sign anything.

items.question: Can I stay in my home during construction?
items.answer: In most cases, yes. For kitchen and bathroom remodels, we set up dust barriers and maintain clean access to the rest of the home. For larger whole-home renovations, we will discuss whether temporary relocation makes sense during the planning phase. We always prioritize minimizing disruption to your daily routine.

items.question: Do you offer financing?
items.answer: Yes. We partner with GreenSky to offer flexible financing options for qualified homeowners. Plans include deferred interest and fixed monthly payment options. We can walk you through the available plans during your consultation or proposal review.

items.question: What happens if something unexpected comes up during construction?
items.answer: If we discover something behind a wall or under a floor that was not visible during the initial assessment, such as outdated wiring, water damage, or code violations, we contact you that same day. We explain what we found, present your options with associated costs, and do not proceed until you approve the change in writing.
